title: Strings - text that can fold values in
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: Strings hold text. An f-string (f"...") lets you fold variables straight into the text with {braces}.
explanation: Gluing text together with + works but gets ugly fast. An f-string reads the way you would say it: put an f before the opening quote, and anything inside {braces} becomes its value. The f is the whole trick - without it, the braces are just braces.
code_example:
name = "Aria"
streak = 4
line = f"{name} showed up {streak} days in a row"
print(line)

quiz.question:
One character makes the braces come alive. Which letter goes before the quote?

greeting = _"hello, {name}"

hints: It is a single letter, sitting right before the opening quote.
